title: EU Cross-Reference Index for Maltese Law
title_en: EU Cross-Reference Index for Maltese Law
description: This index documents the cross-references between Maltese national law and European Union legislation in the areas of data protection, cybersecurity, electronic communications, electronic commerce, and digital innovation. Malta, as an EU Member State, implements EU directives through domestic legislation and applies EU regulations directly. This index facilitates navigation between Maltese implementing measures and the underlying Union instruments.

provisions.title: GDPR Implementation
provisions.content: The Data Protection Act (Cap. 586) implements Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation) in Malta. The Act designates the Information and Data Protection Commissioner as the national supervisory authority under Article 51 GDPR, establishes national derogations and specifications permitted under GDPR Chapter IX, and provides for administrative sanctions in accordance with Article 83 GDPR. Malta applies GDPR directly as an EU regulation, with the national Act complementing rather than re-enacting the GDPR's core provisions.

provisions.title: NIS Directive Transposition
provisions.content: S.L. 586.07 (Network and Information Systems Regulations) transposes Directive (EU) 2016/1148 (NIS Directive) and, following amendment, aligns with Directive (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2 Directive) requirements. The Regulations designate the Malta Information Technology Agency (MITA) as the national competent authority for digital infrastructure and digital service providers, and establish the Malta Computer Emergency Response Team (MALTACERT) as the national CSIRT. Operators of essential services in sectors specified in the Regulations are subject to security and incident notification requirements.

provisions.title: Electronic Communications Framework
provisions.content: The Electronic Communications (Regulation) Act (Cap. 399) transposes the EU regulatory framework for electronic communications, including Directive 2002/21/EC (Framework Directive), Directive 2002/20/EC (Authorisation Directive), Directive 2002/19/EC (Access Directive), and Directive 2002/22/EC (Universal Service Directive), as replaced by the European Electronic Communications Code (Directive (EU) 2018/1972). The Act designates the Malta Communications Authority as the national regulatory authority and establishes the general authorisation framework for electronic communications providers.

provisions.title: eCommerce Directive Transposition
provisions.content: The Electronic Commerce Act (Cap. 426) transposes Directive 2000/31/EC (E-Commerce Directive) into Maltese law, establishing the legal framework for information society services, including liability exemptions for intermediary service providers, requirements for commercial communications, and rules on electronic contracts. The Act is complemented by applicable provisions of the Digital Services Act (Regulation (EU) 2022/2065), which applies directly in Malta and supersedes parts of the E-Commerce Directive.

provisions.title: eIDAS Implementation
provisions.content: Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 (eIDAS) applies directly in Malta. The Malta Communications Authority is designated as the supervisory body responsible for the supervision of qualified trust service providers established in Malta, and maintains the national trusted list. The Electronic Commerce Act and related secondary legislation establish procedural requirements for the notification of electronic identification schemes and for the acceptance of trust services by public sector bodies.

provisions.title: Digital Innovation and Technology
provisions.content: The Malta Digital Innovation Authority Act (Cap. 591) establishes MDIA as the regulator for innovative technology arrangements and services, operating in a manner complementary to applicable Union law including the AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) and the Data Act (Regulation (EU) 2023/2854). The Virtual Financial Assets Act (Cap. 590) established an early national framework for crypto-asset services prior to the application of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCA), which now provides the Union-wide framework.

provisions.title: Critical Infrastructure Protection
provisions.content: Malta's Critical Infrastructure Protection Regulations transpose Council Directive 2008/114/EC on the identification and designation of European critical infrastructures and align with Directive (EU) 2022/2557 on the resilience of critical entities (CER Directive). The Regulations designate the Office of the Prime Minister as the competent authority for critical infrastructure protection coordination and establish the designation and Operator Security Plan requirements for critical infrastructure operators.

provisions.title: Technology Services Regulation
provisions.content: The Technology Arrangements and Services Act (Cap. 592) establishes the regulatory framework for technology service providers operating in Malta, complementing applicable Union law. The Act interfaces with the Digital Operational Resilience Act (Regulation (EU) 2022/2554, DORA) applicable to financial sector technology service providers, and with the Cyber Resilience Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/2847) requirements for ICT products and services.

provisions.title: Freedom of Information
provisions.content: The Freedom of Information Act (Cap. 496) establishes the right of public access to official documents held by public authorities, operating alongside Regulation (EU) 1049/2001 regarding public access to European Parliament, Council, and Commission documents, applicable to those Union institutions. The Act is consistent with the principle of transparency enshrined in Article 15 TFEU and with applicable data protection rules under GDPR governing the disclosure of documents containing personal data.

provisions.title: Consumer Protection in Digital Services
provisions.content: Maltese consumer protection law implementing Directive 2011/83/EU (Consumer Rights Directive), Directive 2005/29/EC (Unfair Commercial Practices Directive), and applicable provisions of the Digital Content Directive (Directive (EU) 2019/770) and the Sale of Goods Directive (Directive (EU) 2019/771) applies in conjunction with the Electronic Commerce Act in relation to online transactions. The Malta Competition and Consumer Affairs Authority enforces consumer protection requirements in the digital environment.

provisions.title: Blockchain and Distributed Ledger Technology
provisions.content: Malta was among the first EU Member States to introduce specific legislation for blockchain and distributed ledger technology, with the Virtual Financial Assets Act, the Malta Digital Innovation Authority Act, and the Innovative Technology Arrangements and Services Act constituting the original 'blockchain triptych'. These national frameworks have been progressively aligned with Union law developments including MiCA and the DLT Pilot Regime (Regulation (EU) 2022/858), which now provide the primary regulatory framework in their respective domains.

provisions.title: Cybersecurity Certification
provisions.content: The EU Cybersecurity Act (Regulation (EU) 2019/881) applies directly in Malta, establishing the European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA) mandate and the European cybersecurity certification framework. Malta participates in the development and implementation of European cybersecurity certification schemes through MITA's cybersecurity function and through Malta's participation in the European Cybersecurity Certification Group. Nationally, MITA coordinates the adoption of applicable cybersecurity certification schemes.

provisions.title: Open Data and Data Reuse
provisions.content: Directive (EU) 2019/1024 on open data and the re-use of public sector information has been transposed into Maltese law, establishing the framework for making public sector information available for reuse. Malta maintains an open data portal providing access to government datasets, and MITA coordinates the implementation of open data policies across public authorities. The framework interfaces with applicable provisions of the Data Governance Act (Regulation (EU) 2022/868) governing data intermediation services and altruistic data sharing.

provisions.title: Electronic Government and Single Digital Gateway
provisions.content: Regulation (EU) 2018/1724 establishing the Single Digital Gateway applies directly in Malta, requiring that specified administrative procedures be made available online and accessible through the common Union gateway. MITA coordinates Malta's participation in the Single Digital Gateway and related Union digital government initiatives, including the European Identity Wallet framework under Regulation (EU) 2024/1183 amending the eIDAS Regulation. Malta's e-government framework is designed to be interoperable with Union digital infrastructure.

provisions.title: Artificial Intelligence Regulation
provisions.content: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act) applies directly in Malta and establishes the Union framework for trustworthy artificial intelligence. MDIA, as Malta's national competent authority for innovative technology, is expected to play a role in the national implementation of the AI Act alongside other sectoral regulators. Malta's prior experience in developing the MDIA framework for technology governance informs its approach to AI regulation, with national implementing measures to complement the directly applicable Union framework.
